headline: Micron sinks 6% in wild week
detail: Chip stocks are under serious pressure, with Micron dropping 6% to cap a brutal week. This confirms the semiconductor weakness that's already hitting your AVGO (SELL, -16%) and weighing on SMH, your only winning position. If the chip narrative keeps deteriorating, SMH's cushion shrinks fast.

headline: Oracle worst week since dot-com bust on AI financing fears
detail: AI infrastructure spending is being questioned at the highest level — Oracle's collapse signals investors are losing faith in the AI capex story. AVGO and PLTR are both levered to that same AI-spending thesis, so this is a direct headwind to your two biggest SELL-flagged names.

headline: Fed's Kashkari projects one rate hike this year
detail: A rate hike projection crushes the multiple-expansion story for high-growth tech and chip names. Higher rates hit margin traders doubly hard — your cost of carry goes up while your positions go down. This macro shift supports the SELL/TRIM signals across your book.

headline: Healthcare stocks becoming haven as investors ditch tech
detail: Sector rotation is real and active — money is physically leaving tech for healthcare. With your book 90% concentrated in tech and chips, you have no natural hedge. This rotation adds selling pressure on top of the stock-specific weakness already showing in AVGO and PLTR.
